seoTitle: How to Import Excel Spreadsheets into ClickUp
metaDescription: Learn how to prepare an Excel file and import it into ClickUp, mapping columns to task fields and matching team members to assignees.
intro: If your task list currently lives in Excel, ClickUp's import wizard can bring it over without retyping everything by hand. This guide covers formatting your spreadsheet's columns, uploading the file through the native import tool, and mapping headers like task name and due date to the right ClickUp fields, including matching team member names to actual workspace users.

steps.heading: Prepare your Excel spreadsheet
steps.body: Structure your rows and label your core column headers clearly before starting the import.

steps.heading: Open the Import & Export settings
steps.body: Click your profile avatar, open Settings, and navigate to Import & Export, then click Start Import.

steps.heading: Select the file format
steps.body: Choose the Excel or CSV format option and drag your saved spreadsheet file into the upload area.

steps.heading: Choose your destination
steps.body: Select the target Space, Folder, or List where the imported tasks should be created.

steps.heading: Map columns to fields
steps.body: Use the interactive mapping grid to connect spreadsheet columns to ClickUp fields and priorities.

steps.heading: Match assignees and verify results
steps.body: Map team member names to active workspace users so assignees transfer correctly, then review the success screen to confirm your list populated correctly.

faq.question: Does ClickUp accept native Excel files or only CSV?
faq.answer: The import wizard supports both Excel and CSV format uploads, so you don't necessarily need to convert your file first.

faq.question: What if my spreadsheet has extra columns I don't need in ClickUp?
faq.answer: You can simply leave those columns unmapped during the import process rather than assigning them to a ClickUp field.

faq.question: Will task assignees transfer automatically from my spreadsheet?
faq.answer: Only if the names or emails in your file match active workspace users, which is why mapping that field carefully matters.

faq.question: Is it a good idea to test with a small file first?
faq.answer: Yes, running a small sample import helps confirm your column mapping is correct before committing to a large spreadsheet upload.
